← Back to Christmas 2023 (Guayaquil/Cuenca/Baltimore)
2023-12-08 Ecuador

Ecuador - Christmas 2023 (Guayaquil/Cuenca/Baltimore)

Christmas 2023 - Ecuador

Our first day in Ecuador, there was a Science and Math fair at Sebastián's old high school. The math club invited him to come to the fair and talk to the kids who are currently in the math club. It was fun to see how excited all the kids were to meet him - he is their celebrity!

Math Club
Sebastián and Romnie, his trainer

Celebrations

As many family and friends weren't able to come to our wedding, we were grateful to have time to celebrate with everyone while in Ecuador.

Celebrating with Sebastián's mom's side of the family
Celebrating with Sebastián's mom's side of the family
Celebrating with Sebastián's dad's side of the family
Celebrating with Sebastián's dad's side of the family

Time with friends
Playing games with friends
More time with friends

Food

Two of Rachel's favorite Ecuadorian foods. Bolon (mashed plantain) and blackberry juice. A great breakfast!
Humitas - another favourite
One of the crabs, escaping. We had crab a number of times - also delicious.
At a restaurant

Cuenca

We travelled to Cuenca for a few days, a city about 3 hours away in the mountains.

We stopped just outside the city and got these beautiful photos!
Just outside Cuenca
In the centre of Cuenca
We did a walking tour of Cuenca and stopped in this beautiful building. It used to be a university and now it has offices for judges.
We also stopped at this colonial house.
During our tour, we got to climb up the tower of one of the basilicas.
With the basilica in the background
A church in Cuenca had a huge nativity display. Lots of little ancient Jewish houses and thousands of figurines. The biggest nativity display in Latin America. In this picture you can see the temple. On the left Mary and Joseph are presenting Jesus on the 8th day. On the right Jesus is talking to the leaders at age 12.
At the nativity display. You can see one of the clouds there. They would periodically thunder and lightning and drop rain!
At dinner - we all have our blackberry juice!
A cute restaurant where we had breakfast one morning. It was filled with plants and knick knacks.
At an outlook over the city. Sebastián and Rachel rode the bike across the wire. Kind of scary, but a lot of fun!
At an outlook over the city. The floor was made of glass here too!! Beautiful views.
On our drive back from Cuenca, we stopped in Las Cajas, a park with many lakes. It was peaceful and super gorgeous.

Back in Guayaquil

Back in Guayaquil, Rachel spent a lot of time looking at old photos with Sebastián's parents. This is baby Sebastián with his mom.
We got to spend a lot of time with family. This is with some of Sebastián's mom's side of the family after we had them over for crabs.

Christmas

We celebrated Christmas at Sebastián's aunt's house.
With the family!
Sebastián brought fireworks. We had a lot of fun with the kids there.
More fireworks!
Rachel and Sebastián's cousin, Elizabeth
Sebastián and Rachel

Años Viejos

In Ecuador on New Year's Eve, it's a tradition to buy a paper mache character which you then burn on a huge bonfire. The characters are called Años Viejos (meaning Old Year), and burning them represents getting rid of the bad things from the last year to start fresh in the new year. On one of our last days in Ecuador, we went downtown to see the Años Viejos!

Rachel and Giovanna with the Años Viejos. There is also a competition for the best giant Año Viejo. We went to see a bunch of the gigantic ones on display.
Rachel and Giovanna got coconut juice!
